title: Root Walk
desc: The footpath over the Great Tree's broad roots has gone slick with moss again, and the guide cords are no better.

steps.text:
Around the Great Tree, the roots are too large and too old to route paths around them. The paths go over instead: board strips laid into the broader roots, guide cords tied between trunks, and little stepping stones where the ground dips under fern and moss. It is a good system until the grove remembers it is still a forest and starts reclaiming the whole arrangement one damp green inch at a time.

The path keeper leans on a scraper and looks down the slick stretch ahead. "Scrape the moss from the root boards if you want the walkway," she says. "Or retie the guide cords between the trunks if you prefer the side line. Either way, people come here hoping for insight. A twisted ankle is a very low form of revelation."

options.label: Scrape the moss from the root-board walkway
Retie the guide cords between the trunks

steps.text:
The moss comes free in damp green ribbons, exposing the pale worn grain of the boards beneath and the run of old root bark on either side. You work section by section, scraping the walkway back to clean traction and brushing the loosened growth into baskets before it can reattach itself out of principle. By the end, the path no longer gleams with danger. It simply curves where the roots insist it must.

A scholar crosses moments later carrying more papers than balance usually permits and reaches the far side without incident. The keeper seems pleased by this modest triumph. "Good. People can now return to pretending their minds are what brought them here safely."

options.label: Carry the moss basket aside and leave the path secure

steps.text:
The guide cords hang at hand height where the ground undulates too much for confidence to remain an appropriate strategy. Two have sagged, one has wrapped itself around a trunk knot, and another was tied at some point by a person who believed symmetry was optional. You untie the whole unhappy section and pull it back into even runs from tree to tree until the line guides a hand naturally instead of starting a small argument with it.

When you test the finished section, the cords now lead around the broad root swell in one clear motion. The grove beyond remains layered with shadow, bark, and quiet suggestion, but the way through it has become honest again. The keeper runs her fingers down the taut line and nods. "Better. A sacred place still benefits from competent rope."

options.label: Tuck the spare fiber away and leave the guide line true
